chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P.No.D-343 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of MA No.2517 of 2025 03.12.2025 Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi, AAG along with Snobar Ali Sipio Mukhtiarkar (HBM), Tapedar Shahid Raza and Muhammad Ali ADC-I, Mirpurkhas. Through this petition, the petitioner seeks issuance of certified true copy of Revenue Entry No. 3428 dated 04.05.1989. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that several applications have been filed for obtaining certified true copy of the record of rights, the latest of which was submitted on 08.04.2025 to respondent No.3. Pursuant to that application, respondent No.3 called for report from the concerned Mukhtiarkar. Learned counsel refers to page 35 of the case file, containing a letter issued by the Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas, addressed to Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Taluka Hussain Bux Mari, directing submission of the requisite report. It is further argued that the report from Mukhtiarkar, as required, is also available at page 35. However, despite receipt of report, no appropriate orders were passed by the Deputy Commissioner and the petitioner’s application remained pending. Consequently, this Court, vide order dated 21.05.2025, disposed of the petition in the following terms: “Through this petition, the petitioner seeks issuance of a certified true copy of Entry No. 3424 dated 04-05-1989. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that multiple applications have been filed to obtain a certified true copy of the record of rights, the most recent being the application dated 08-04-2025 filed before respondent No. 3, who thereafter called for a report from the concerned Mukhtiarkar.

Consequently, this Court, vide order dated 21.05.2025, disposed of the petition in the following terms: “Through this petition, the petitioner seeks issuance of a certified true copy of Entry No. 3424 dated 04-05-1989. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that multiple applications have been filed to obtain a certified true copy of the record of rights, the most recent being the application dated 08-04-2025 filed before respondent No. 3, who thereafter called for a report from the concerned Mukhtiarkar. Reference is made to page 35 of the file containing a letter issued by the Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas to the Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Taluka Hussain Bux Mari. It is contended that although the report has been submitted, no order has been passed on the petitioner‟s application. Learned AAG, present in Court, waives notice and submits that under Notification dated 21-10-2025, the Senior Member Board of Revenue Sindh has established a „Public Grievances Redressal Cell‟ headed by the concerned Deputy Commissioner for dealing with matters relating to land demarcation, Foti Khata Badal, partition, and other land-related issues. He assures that if the petitioner approaches the said Cell, the matter shall be decided in accordance with law after providing an opportunity of hearing. Counsel for the petitioner, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the petition. Accordingly, the petition was disposed of with directions to the Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas, to decide the petitioner‟s pending application strictly in accordance with law, after hearing all concerned parties, within 30 days. Compliance was directed to be reported to this Court through the Additional Registrar.” Today, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks initiation of contempt proceedings against the alleged contemnors. This request has been opposed by learned AAG, who has placed on record the compliance report submitted by Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas. The report states as follows: “In compliance with the order dated 21.05.2025 passed by the Honourable High Court of Sindh, Circuit Court, Mirpurkhas, requiring submission of a compliance report after decision by the Public Grievances Redressal Cell (PGRC), it is respectfully submitted that the relevant land/shop record, including registered sale deeds, previous title documents, and record of rights, was thoroughly examined.

This request has been opposed by learned AAG, who has placed on record the compliance report submitted by Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas. The report states as follows: “In compliance with the order dated 21.05.2025 passed by the Honourable High Court of Sindh, Circuit Court, Mirpurkhas, requiring submission of a compliance report after decision by the Public Grievances Redressal Cell (PGRC), it is respectfully submitted that the relevant land/shop record, including registered sale deeds, previous title documents, and record of rights, was thoroughly examined. It was deemed appropriate to issue necessary clarification to the parties. Accordingly, Mukhtiarkar Revenue, Hussain Bux Mari, was directed vide letter No. ADC-I/PGRC/24 dated 18.07.2025 to issue the requisite rubkari (Annex-A). The Mukhtiarkar has complied and issued rubkari No. MUKH/HBM/766 dated 30.07.2025 in accordance with the official record. Thereafter, the petitioner instituted Civil Suit No. 74 of 2025 before the Court of the IInd Senior Civil Judge, Mirpurkhas, concerning the same subject matter. Copies of summons and plaint are enclosed. A detailed compliance report was earlier forwarded to the Additional Registrar, High Court of Sindh, Circuit Court, Mirpurkhas, vide letter No. ADC-I/MPS/PGRC/31 dated 17.09.2025 (Annex-B). Since the matter is now sub judice before the Civil Court and the directions of this Honourable Court have been fully complied with, it is humbly prayed that the contempt notice issued to Mr. Rasheed Masood Khan, Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas, may kindly be vacated.” Learned AAG reiterates the request that the contempt notice against the Deputy Commissioner be discharged. In view of the above submissions and the compliance placed on record, the contempt application stands disposed of accordingly and the contempt notice issued against the Deputy Commissioner is vacated. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa,
